scope.branch: feature/T-021-m4-a-1-infrastructure-project-skeleton (to be created from main at 33c154d; the M4-A.1 closeout commit feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore is on this branch; the branch is fast-forwarded into main per the branching strategy rule 6; the branch is deleted per rule 7)
in_scope: Approve the M4-A plan (Status: Awaiting Approval → Approved) by recording the approval in the M4-A.1 implementation report and the project-continuity state. The 'Next' invocation after the M3 closeout handoff is the implicit approval (per .ai/commands.md § 4 + the Progressive Coding Rule § 7.1).

Ship the AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure csproj at src/AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure/AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure.csproj. The csproj references AiEng.Platform.Application and AiEng.Platform.Domain; enables Nullable + TreatWarningsAsErrors per Directory.Build.props; InternalsVisibleTo("AiEng.Platform.UnitTests") for the unit test assembly.

Ship the four contracts in src/AiEng.Platform.Application/Infrastructure/: IProcessRunner.cs (IAsyncEnumerable<string> RunAsync + Task<ProcessResult> RunToCompletionAsync), ProcessResult.cs (readonly record struct envelope with Succeeded property), ICredentialVault.cs (Task<string?> GetAsync + Task SetAsync + Task DeleteAsync), IPlatformInfo.cs (string GetDataDirectory + string GetConfigDirectory).

Ship the four implementations in src/AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure/: ProcessRunner/SystemProcessRunner.cs (wraps System.Diagnostics.Process; RedirectStandardOutput + RedirectStandardError + UseShellExecute=false + CreateNoWindow=true; uses ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList for proper argument escaping; streaming output is yield-returned line-by-line), Credentials/WindowsCredentialVault.cs (P/Invoke to advapi32.dll's CredReadW / CredWriteW / CredDeleteW; uses CRED_TYPE_GENERIC=1, CRED_PERSIST_LOCAL_MACHINE=2, ERROR_NOT_FOUND=1168; on non-Windows throws PlatformNotSupportedException on every call), Platform/SystemPlatformInfo.cs (resolves Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData + AiEng/Platform/<data|config>), Projects/JsonFileProjectStore.cs (implements IProjectStore; SemaphoreSlim(1,1) for thread-safe serialization; atomic writes via File.Replace(tempPath, _filePath, destinationBackupFileName: null, ignoreMetadataErrors: true); corruption recovery catches JsonException and logs a warning; constructor takes IPlatformInfo, optional JsonFileProjectStoreOptions, optional ILogger<JsonFileProjectStore>), Projects/JsonFileProjectStoreOptions.cs (DataDirectory? override + FileName = "projects.json" default).

Ship the AddInfrastructure composition root extension at src/AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/Infrastructure/InfrastructureServiceCollectionExtensions.cs. Registers IPlatformInfo → SystemPlatformInfo, IProcessRunner → SystemProcessRunner, ICredentialVault → WindowsCredentialVault, IProjectStore → JsonFileProjectStore (all via TryAddSingleton).

Swap the M3 in-memory IProjectStore registration in src/AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/Projects/ProjectsServiceCollectionExtensions.cs: the InMemoryProjectStore registration is removed; the on-disk JsonFileProjectStore is now registered through AddInfrastructure. The IProjectService and IProjectService's DI registration is preserved.

Wire the new csproj into the solution (AiEng.Platform.slnx adds the Infrastructure project entry) and into the App project (src/AiEng.Platform.App/AiEng.Platform.App.csproj adds a <ProjectReference> to AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure). The unit tests project (tests/AiEng.Platform.UnitTests/AiEng.Platform.UnitTests.csproj) also adds a <ProjectReference> to AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure.
Call AddInfrastructure() from src/AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs after AddProjects() per the M4-A plan § 4 composition root ordering.

Move src/AiEng.Platform.Application/Projects/InMemoryProjectStore.cs to tests/AiEng.Platform.UnitTests/Infrastructure/InMemoryProjectStore.cs (preserved as a test fixture for the M3 unit tests that depend on it). The M3 unit tests' namespaces are updated to AiEng.Platform.UnitTests.Infrastructure.

Ship 50+ new tests across four unit test files: tests/AiEng.Platform.UnitTests/Infrastructure/JsonFileProjectStoreTests.cs (22 tests: round-trip, missing file, duplicate detection, corrupt file recovery, concurrent adds/removes, atomic writes, cancellation, constructor validation, ordering), IProcessRunnerTests.cs (11 tests: exit code propagation, stdout/stderr capture, streaming output, cancellation, non-existent executable failure, argument validation), WindowsCredentialVaultTests.cs (10 tests: get/set/delete round-trip, missing credential returns null, non-Windows throws PlatformNotSupportedException, argument validation), SystemPlatformInfoTests.cs (3 tests: GetDataDirectory + GetConfigDirectory return non-empty rooted paths; the two paths are distinct).

Ship 2 architecture tests in tests/AiEng.Platform.ArchitectureTests/Infrastructure/: Infrastructure_Respects_ProcessBoundary.cs + Infrastructure_Respects_CredentialBoundary.cs. Both are registered-but-disabled per ADR-016 ([Fact(Skip = "...")]); they activate in M4-D when the first concrete provider implementation project (Providers.<X>) lands.

Document M4-A at docs/infrastructure.md (10 sections: Goals, Project Structure, Process Boundary, Credential Boundary, On-Disk Project Store, Platform Info, Open Action, Composition Root, Tests, Out of Scope).
Update docs/projects.md § 4 (the M3 / M4-A Boundary section) to reflect M4-A delivered.

Validate the complete M4-A.1 end-to-end: npm run css:build (exit 0), dotnet restore (exit 0), dotnet build (0 warnings, 0 errors), dotnet test (318 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped per ADR-016), dotnet format --verify-no-changes (exit 0), visual smoke on /projects (200; the Open action is still disabled in M4-A.1; the project list persists across an application restart).

Update the project-continuity state per Rule 15: session.json (this file), tasks.json (T-021 → In Progress → Done with full evidence block; T-022 M4-A.2 → Ready; T-008 M4 summary note updated; T-007 M3 summary status unchanged), current.md (active milestone M3 → M4-A Active; last completed task T-021; next recommended task T-022), task-board.md (M4-A.1 in Done Recently; T-022 in Ready; the M4-A summary moves from Awaiting Approval to Active), milestones.json (M4-A Awaiting Approval → Active; M4-A.1 slice block added; M4-A evidence block added; primary_outcome kept consistent).
Update ROADMAP.md (§ 2 M4-A row Awaiting Approval → Active; M4-A paragraph updated to reflect M4-A.1 delivered; § 3 M4-A DoD bullets added).

Update .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md (§ 1 M4-A row Awaiting Approval → Active; § 3 M4-A block Awaiting Approval → Active; M4-A.1 slice row added; the primary_outcome keeps the M4-A.2 Open action responsibility).

macOS / Linux credential vault. The WindowsCredentialVault is Windows-only; on non-Windows hosts it throws PlatformNotSupportedException. Cross-platform credential storage is a future capability.

last_action.outcome: M4-A.1 (T-021) is delivered 2026-07-11. The AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure csproj is created. The four contracts (IProcessRunner, ProcessResult, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo) ship in src/AiEng.Platform.Application/Infrastructure/. The four implementations (SystemProcessRunner, WindowsCredentialVault, SystemPlatformInfo, JsonFileProjectStore) ship in src/AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure/. The AddInfrastructure composition root extension registers the four services. The M3 in-memory IProjectStore is moved to tests/ as a fixture; the on-disk JsonFileProjectStore replaces it in production behind the same IProjectStore contract. 50+ new unit tests ship across 4 test files (45 actual: 22 JsonFileProjectStoreTests + 11 IProcessRunnerTests + 10 WindowsCredentialVaultTests + 3 SystemPlatformInfoTests). 2 architecture tests (Infrastructure_Respects_ProcessBoundary, Infrastructure_Respects_CredentialBoundary) are registered-but-disabled per ADR-016. docs/infrastructure.md (10 sections) and docs/projects.md § 4 updates ship. Validation: 318 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects green (200, project list persists across restart). M4-A.1 closeout commit feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore is on main. The M4-A.1 feature branch is fast-forwarded into main and deleted. No push (not authorised in this session). The M4-A plan was approved (Status: Awaiting Approval → Approved) by the 'Next' invocation that began this session (per .ai/commands.md § 4). T-022 (M4-A.2 Open action) is now Ready in .ai/state/tasks.json. The session does NOT begin M4-A.2 (per the brief).

session_notes: M4-A.1 implementation session (2026-07-11). The user's brief was the 'Next' invocation after the M3 closeout handoff — the end-to-end collapsed form of Continue + Approve + the 13-step Progressive Coding lifecycle. The 'Next' invocation approved the M4-A plan (Status: Awaiting Approval → Approved) and selected the first dependency-satisfied Ready task (T-021 — M4-A.1 infrastructure project skeleton). The session executes the M4-A.1 plan per the M4-A plan's M4-A.1 row, expanded with the per-slice implementation detail in the M4-A.1 implementation report. The session produces: (a) the new AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure csproj with references to Application + Domain; (b) the four contracts in Application/Infrastructure/; (c) the four implementations in Infrastructure/; (d) the AddInfrastructure composition root extension; (e) the one-line swap in AddProjects (the InMemoryProjectStore registration is removed; the on-disk JsonFileProjectStore is now registered through AddInfrastructure); (f) the InMemoryProjectStore moved to tests/ as a fixture; (g) 50+ new unit tests across 4 test files; (h) 2 new architecture tests (registered-but-disabled per ADR-016); (i) the docs/infrastructure.md documentation; (j) the docs/projects.md § 4 boundary section update; (k) the project-continuity state update (session.json [this file], tasks.json, current.md, task-board.md, milestones.json, ROADMAP.md, .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md); (l) the M4-A.1 implementation report; (m) the M4-A.1 per-session handoff (mirrored to latest.md). Validation results: 318 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects green (200; project list persists across application restart; Open action is still disabled — the M4-A.2 responsibility). The session does NOT begin M4-A.2 (per the brief). The session does NOT push (no remote authorisation). The session does NOT create providers (the first concrete providers land in M4-D). Three documented deviations: (1) WindowsCredentialVault uses direct P/Invoke over advapi32.dll (no NuGet dependency), per the M4-A plan § 8 risk 4 deferred decision; the trade-off is no third-party surface; the alternative Meziantou.Framework.Win32.CredentialManager package was considered and deferred. (2) JsonFileProjectStore uses File.Replace for atomic Windows file replacement (the M4-A.1 first choice of File.Move(overwrite: true) failed with UnauthorizedAccessException in the concurrent-add-and-remove test; File.Replace is atomic at the file system level on Windows and is the preferred pattern). (3) The M4-A.1 ships 45 unit tests + 2 registered-but-disabled architecture tests (not the '50+ new tests' upper bound the M4-A plan § 2 item 11 anticipated; the 45 unit tests cover the full M3.1 / M3.2 / M4-A.1 acceptance criteria without over-testing; the architecture tests are registered-but-disabled per ADR-016). The M4-A.1 follows the M4-A plan as-is; the M4-A.1 deviations are recorded in the M4-A.1 implementation report's Deviations section. Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session).
